citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
One black and white pig has a large area of hairloss along its back from the shoulder blades to the rump. The skin in this
area is thickened, cracked and flaking. Several areas of reddened skin are located throughout the affected area of the
back. The facility is aware of the pig's condition but has not contacted the attending veterinarian and no treatment has
been provided.
Hair loss and cracked and reddened skin could indicate a serious underlying health issue which could cause pain and
discomfort to the pig and could worsen without intervention.
Each dealer and exhibitor must ensure that there is direct and frequent communication with the attending veterinarian so
that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed. The licensee
must have this pig evaluated by the attending veterinarian for an accurate diagnosis and to develop an effective treatment
plan. The licensee must ensure that all veterinary care concerns are communicated to the attending veterinarian as
quickly as possible.
Correction date: Licensee spoke to the attending veterinarian at the time of the inspection concerning the pig's condition.

In the large pig enclosure, housing at least 24 pigs, there is a section of metal fencing lying on the ground. Additionally,
there are pieces of plastic also scattered on the ground around the pigs' wooden shelter. Debris and trash in the
enclosure could cause injury to the animals should they come into contact with it or consume it.
Provisions shall be made for the removal and disposal of animal and food wastes, bedding, dead animals, trash and
debris. The licensee must remove the metal fence and the pieces of plastic from the pig enclosures. The licensee must
also ensure that all animal enclosures remain free of trash and debris at all times.
Correction date: March 09, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
One black and white pig has a large area of hairloss along its back from the shoulder blades to the rump. The skin in this 
area is thickened, cracked and flaking. Several areas of reddened skin are located throughout the affected area of the 
back. The facility is aware of the pig's condition but has not contacted the attending veterinarian and no treatment has 
been provided.  
 
Hair loss and cracked and reddened skin could indicate a serious underlying health issue which could cause pain and 
discomfort to the pig and could worsen without intervention. 
 
Each dealer and exhibitor must ensure that there is direct and frequent communication with the attending veterinarian so 
that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed. The licensee 
must have this pig evaluated by the attending veterinarian for an accurate diagnosis and to develop an effective treatment 
plan. The licensee must ensure that all veterinary care concerns are communicated to the attending veterinarian as 
quickly as possible.  
 
Correction date: Licensee spoke to the attending veterinarian at the time of the inspection concerning the pig's condition. 
3.125(d) 
   
Facilities, general. 
In the large pig enclosure, housing at least 24 pigs, there is a section of metal fencing lying on the ground. Additionally, 
there are pieces of plastic also scattered on the ground around the pigs' wooden shelter. Debris and trash in the 
enclosure could cause injury to the animals should they come into contact with it or consume it. 
 
Provisions shall be made for the removal and disposal of animal and food wastes, bedding, dead animals, trash and 
debris. The licensee must remove the metal fence and the pieces of plastic from the pig enclosures. The licensee must 
also ensure that all animal enclosures remain free of trash and debris at all times. 
 
Correction date: March 09, 2026

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
